channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Tier 1 geopolitical news headlines curated for global events coverage

item.title: 🛢️ Brent crude hits 4-year high as US-Iran war deadlock keeps supply off market
item.description: Oil prices surged to a four-year high on concerns that the ongoing US-Iran conflict could escalate further, leading to prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 Taiwan tops Beijing agenda for Trump-Xi summit
item.description: The upcoming Trump-Xi meeting in mid-May will have profound implications for Taiwans 23 million people, with the islands status expected to dominate discussions.

item.title: 🇪🇺💰 ECB to hold rates steady but keep hikes on table amid Iran war inflation
item.description: The European Central Bank will keep interest rates unchanged but signal that a rate hike may be needed soon to combat energy-driven price surges from the Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸💵 Fed holds rates steady in most divided vote since 1992
item.description: The Federal Reserve kept its policy rate unchanged in what is likely Jay Powells last meeting as chair, with the board showing its deepest divisions in over three decades over inflation concerns.

item.title: ⚔️🇮🇷 Iran war and Strait of Hormuz stuck in limbo as Trump mulls Iranian offer
item.description: Oil prices continue rising as the Trump administration considers an Iranian proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while delaying nuclear talks.

item.title: 🇨🇳✈️ Chinese military experts analyze how Iran detected and damaged US F-35 stealth jet
item.description: SCMP reports Chinese military analysts have mapped out how Iranian air defenses were able to detect and damage an F-35 stealth fighter during combat operations, potentially revealing vulnerabilities in the advanced aircraft.

item.title: 🇧🇾🇷🇺 Belarus bars Russian conscripts from leaving country as Ukraine war continues
item.description: Minsk has prohibited conscripted Russian soldiers and those from occupied Ukrainian territories from leaving the country, potentially preventing escape from military service.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran War Exposes Big Oil Transatlantic Divide
item.description: European oil majors posted strong first-quarter profits from trading gains as the Iran war disrupted supply chains, highlighting how shifting barrels can trump pumping them. The conflict has created different opportunities for European traders vs American drillers.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Deploys Fiber-Optic Drones Against Israel
item.description: Hezbollah has begun using fiber-optic controlled drones that evade electronic jamming, a technology widely used in the Ukraine war. The thin cable connecting operators directly to drones makes them impossible to electronically jam.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Ramps Up Trade Leverage Before Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Beijing announced new trade rules that could punish foreign companies for reducing reliance on Chinese supply chains, alarming US businesses weeks before the Trump-Xi summit. Analysts say this could undercut American efforts to reduce supply chain dependence on China.

item.title: 🇲🇱🇷🇺⚔️ Mali Setbacks Challenge Russian Influence in Africa
item.description: Reversals suffered by Mali's Moscow-backed military government have dented Russia's image as a security guarantor in Africa and threaten its strategic and mineral interests on the continent.

item.title: 🇨🇳💻🤖 DeepSeek Bets on Huawei as China Seeks to Replace Nvidia
item.description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek has aligned with Huawei as Beijing pushes to end reliance on Western technology, signaling a major shift in China's AI semiconductor strategy.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️🏛️ UAE Reviews Multilateral Ties After OPEC Exit
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is reassessing its role across multilateral organizations following its withdrawal from OPEC, but is not considering further departures, a UAE official said.

item.title: 🇲🇽🇺🇸⚖️ US Indicts 10 Mexican Officials for Drug Trafficking
item.description: The governor of Sinaloa and nine other current and former Mexican officials were charged in a US indictment with drug trafficking and weapons offenses, accused of aiding massive narcotics imports.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇷🇺🇺🇦 Trump and Putin Discuss Cease-Fire in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin spoke about a potential temporary cease-fire in Ukraine, with Putin offering help on Iran but Trump pushing focus on Ukraine peace efforts.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇺🇸⚓ South Korea to Lead Joint Maritime Drill With US for First Time
item.description: South Korea will lead multinational naval forces for the first time in a US-led maritime exercise starting in June, marking a significant step in defense cooperation amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇮🇳🚢 Japan Shares Guarded Mogami Warship Design With India in Defense Boost
item.description: Tokyo has offered Delhi the plans to its $500-million Mogami-class warship so that it can be built in India using local industry, marking a major defense technology transfer between the two nations.

item.title: 📰📉🌍 World Press Freedom Declines as Authoritarianism Rises Globally
item.description: Three in four countries now have press freedom rated "problematic" or worse, with the 2026 World Press Freedom Index painting a bleak picture as authoritarian governance expands worldwide.

item.title: 🚢 US seeks international coalition to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio approved the creation of the Maritime Freedom Construct (MFC), a joint State Department-Pentagon initiative to restore freedom of navigation. The move follows months of disrupted shipping as Iran blockaded the strait in response to US-Israeli attacks.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German Chancellor Merz says relations with Trump remain good despite Iran spat
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz downplayed tensions with Washington over Germany not sending naval vessels to Hormuz, while warning of economic fallout from the Iran conflict. Trump had criticized Germany and NATO allies for not supporting the maritime operation.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Bank of England expected to hold rates as Iran war clouds economic outlook
item.description: The UK central bank is set to keep interest rates unchanged, awaiting clearer signals on the economic impact of the Iran conflict. Markets are watching for any hints of future rate moves amid oil-driven inflation pressures.

item.title: 🛢️ Brent crude surges past on stalled Iran talks
item.description: Oil prices jumped more than 6% as negotiations between Washington and Tehran showed no signs of progress. The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, with analysts warning prices could climb further if the conflict escalates.

item.title: 🌍 Countries conclude first fossil fuel phase-out conference in Colombia
item.description: Delegates from 56 nations wrapped up the Santa Marta conference on transitioning away from fossil fuels. No binding commitments emerged, but participants highlighted financing gaps for developing nations and announced the next meeting will be in Tuvalu.

item.title: 💰 US War in Iran Has Cost $25 Billion So Far, Pentagon Official Says
item.description: A senior Pentagon official provided the first official estimate of the military price tag for the Iran conflict, an amount equal to NASA's entire annual budget.

item.title: ⚖️ UN Reports Iran Executed 21 and Arrested 4,000 Since War Began
item.description: Iran has executed at least 21 people and arrested over 4,000 on national security charges since the US-Israel conflict began, according to UN human rights chief Volker Turk.
